cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The subatomic particle with a positive charge found in the nucleus

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The subatomic particle with no charge found in the nucleus

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The subatomic particle with a negative charge found outside the nucleus

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The number of protons in the nucleus of an atom, which defines its element

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The total number of protons plus neutrons in the nucleus

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The weighted average mass of all naturally occurring isotopes of an element

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The formula $\overline{m} = \sum (\text{isotope mass} \times \text{fractional abundance})$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The amount of substance containing $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ particles

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Avogadro's number
cards.answer: $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ — the number of representative particles in one mole of a substance

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $n = \frac{m}{M}$ where $m$ is mass in grams and $M$ is molar mass in g/mol

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The mass in grams of one mole of a substance, numerically equal to its formula mass in amu

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The electron configuration principle stating electrons fill the lowest available energy orbitals first

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The rule that no two electrons in the same atom can have identical sets of four quantum numbers

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The rule that electrons occupy separate orbitals of the same subshell before pairing up

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Principal quantum number ($n$)
cards.answer: Describes the energy level and size of an orbital; $n = 1, 2, 3, \ldots$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Angular momentum quantum number ($l$)
cards.answer: Describes the shape of an orbital; $l = 0$ (s), $1$ (p), $2$ (d), $3$ (f)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Magnetic quantum number ($m_l$)
cards.answer: Describes the orientation of an orbital in space; values from $-l$ to $+l$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Spin quantum number ($m_s$)
cards.answer: Describes the spin of an electron; values $+\frac{1}{2}$ or $-\frac{1}{2}$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The region of space where there is a 90% probability of finding an electron

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Effective nuclear charge ($Z_{\text{eff}}$)
cards.answer: The net positive charge experienced by an electron after accounting for shielding by inner electrons; $Z_{\text{eff}} = Z - \sigma$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The reduction of the nuclear charge felt by outer electrons due to inner (core) electrons

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The trend of increasing atomic radius as you move down a group and decreasing across a period

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The energy required to remove the outermost electron from a gaseous atom

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The energy released when a gaseous atom gains an electron

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An atom's ability to attract shared electrons in a covalent bond

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Photoelectron spectroscopy (PES)
cards.answer: Experimental technique that measures ionization energies of electrons in different subshells, revealing electron configuration

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: As you move across Period 3 from Na to Cl, ionization energy generally...
cards.answer: Increases, because $Z_{\text{eff}}$ increases while electrons are added to the same shell

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Electrons in the outermost energy level that participate in bonding

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An atom or ion with an unpaired electron, making it paramagnetic and reactive
